ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions To Trustee
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To: 1. Determine Validity Of Purported Trust; 2. Invalidate The Purported Trust; 3. Impose A Constructive Trust And 4. Additional Causes Of Action Pursuant To P.C. 855
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel T. Bernhard for and with Patrick Steele and Tina Hall, Trustees.
Milla L. Lvovich for and with Alystar Sacks, Petitioner (via Zoom).
James Forbes for Alexandra Bullock, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Amy Harrington for Richard Snider, Trust Protector (via Zoom).
Cory R. Darnell for Office of Attorney General.

The acknowledged a recent change of counsel for petitioners Patrick Steele and Tina Hall and requested an update on the case status. Counsel reported that written discovery is completed but depositions have not been scheduled. The court inquired about the possibility of mediation. The parties expressed a willingness to proceed with mediation. Ms. Harrington suggested that the trust should pay for the cost of mediation.

The court determined that that a Breslin Order was necessary to ensure all parties participate in mediation, and acknowledged that that the cost of mediation should be covered by the trust, with the understanding that this expense is minimal compared to litigation costs.

After discussions, the court set a hearing on Status of Mediation for August 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and continued the underlying petitions to be heard on the same date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust, To Invalidate The Irving B. Sacks Revocable Trust, To Impose A Constructive Trust (Prob. C. 17200(B)(3)(13)), And Additional Causes Of Action Pursuant To Prob C. 855.
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Continued To May-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust, To Invalidate The Irving B. Sacks Revocable Trust, To Impose A Constructive Trust (Prob. C. 17200(B)(3)(13)), And Additional Causes Of Action Pursuant To Prob C. 855.
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Continued To May-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To: 1. Determine Validity Of Purported Trust; 2. Invalidate The Purported Trust; 3. Impose A Constructive Trust And 4. Additional Causes Of Action Pursuant To P.C. 855
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Continued To May-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daphne C. Lin for Patrick S. Steele and Tina Hall, Trustees/Petitioners (via Zoom).
Milla Lvovich for and with Alystar Sacks, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Amy Harrington for Richard Snider, Trust Protector (via Zoom).
James R. Forbes for Alexandra Bullock, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Ms. Harrington stated that she had requested a retainer for the trust protector nearly a year ago, and that the trustees' petition for instructions includes a statement that it is uncertain whether a retainer is allowable, that the fees had to be accrued first. She sent Ms. Lin a bill for $10,000 in February, which was declined by Ms. Lin on behalf of her clients, stating the trust instrument does not clearly allow for direct payments to the trust protector's attorney and that reimbursement should be discretionary.

Ms. Lin countered that their position has not been to deny payment but to seek court clarification regarding whether fees should be paid directly or reimbursed. She further expressed concerns about potentially high litigation costs due to ongoing disputes involving the trust protector and the trust beneficiaries.

The Court inquired whether the trustees themselves had been paid from the trust. Ms. Lin confirmed that her firm had been compensated but could not specify the amount. The Court noted that the trust instrument explicitly provides for the trust protector's expenses to be paid and found it problematic that the trustees were preventing the trust protector from obtaining counsel. The Court observed that this could be grounds for removal of the trustees under the terms of the trust.

The Court authorized payment of a $25,000 retainer from the trust to Attorney Harrington for the trust protector's legal fees.

The parties discussed the potential for mediation, and all agreed that further discovery would be beneficial prior to mediation. The court continued the matter to May 12, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for further progress. At the next hearing, the court will consider whether to issue a case management order or schedule further proceedings.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust, To Invalidate The Irving B. Sacks Revocable Trust, To Impose A Constructive Trust (Prob. C. 17200(B)(3)(13)), And Additional Causes Of Action Pursuant To Prob C. 855.
Off Calendar Nov-25-2024 Continued To Dec-09-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):
Amy Harrington for Richard Snyder, Trust Protector.
Daphne C. Lin for Patrick Steele and Tina Hall, Petitioners.
Cory Darnell for the Department of Justice, representing the People of the State of California.
Sean Fischer for Alystar Sacks, Petitioner.
James R. Forbes for Alexandra Bullock, Petitioner.

Counsel report that no progress has been made in resolving the trust dispute. Discussions are held with regards to the Petition for Instructions and Objections filed by Richard Snyder. It was noted that the Petition to Determine the Validity of the Trust will be addressed after adjudication of the Petition for Instructions, which counsel believes is ready to be decided. The court continues the Petition to Determine Validity of the Trust and Petition for Instructions to Trustee to December 9, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Cory Darnell made a request to other counsel that service of future filings be addressed to him, as the previously served Deputy Attorney General is no long assigned to this matter.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Deputy: Singh; Not reported (D204)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-06-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daphine Lin appeared via zoom for Patrick Steele and Tina Hall (petitioners).
Milla Lvovich appeared via zoom for and with Alystar Sacks (petitioner).
Amy Harrington appeared via zoom for Richard Snider (trust protector).
Cory Darnell appeared via zoom from the Department of Justice.

Ms. Lvovich argued that the trustee displayed self-interest and personal animosity toward the petitioner's client and family, suggesting undue influence and improper behavior. Ms. Lvovich requested the preliminary injunction to allow for discovery on these matters and to prevent the sale of the property. Ms. Lin emphasized the trustee's obligation to protect trust assets, noting the property had been previously broken into. The Court questioned the necessity of an injunction without a bond and argued that halting the sale was not justified based on the evidence presented.

The oral request from Ms. Lvovich to extend the temporary restraining was denied. The Order to Show Cause Why a Preliminary Injunction should not be granted is denied.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Santos. Not reported.
